name: The Falcon Lounge
image_prompt: Interior of the main lounge of the Millennium Falcon spaceship from Star Wars. A curved octagonal room with white-and-gray metal bulkhead walls, exposed pipes, gray padded wall panels. Dark brown leather banquette booth curving around a low circular dark metal Dejarik holographic chess table at the center. A bright blue-green holographic chessboard grid glowing from the tabletop. Two grip-handled seats at the table. A chrome service tray on the seat. A doorway with a rounded rectangular frame on one side leading into a dim corridor with red bulkhead lights. A tool locker and padded gray wall panels on the other side. Cool diffused industrial white interior lighting with amber accent lights recessed around the table. Worn scuffed deck plating under the table. Empty, no people. Colors: off-white bulkhead, brushed chrome silver, dark brown leather, bright holochess cyan teal, amber recessed light. Classic Star Wars original trilogy atmosphere.
location: The main lounge of the Millennium Falcon, a YT-1300 light freighter held together by maintenance miracles and stubborn affection. Currently in hyperspace between jumps, two parsecs out from a safe port. The ship belongs to a smuggler who has left the passenger compartment to the working group and retreated to the cockpit with his co-pilot. The co-pilot is not human.
time: Ship time reads 0314. Hyperspace has no day or night. The crew is on a four-hour sleep cycle and this is the middle of the off-shift.
weather: Climate control set to a cool 20 degrees. The air has the faint metallic edge of recycled ship atmosphere. Humidity is low. The floor plating is warm near the corridor to the engine room and cooler toward the forward bulkhead.

atmosphere.lighting: Diffused cool white overheads from recessed panels along the ceiling seam. Warm amber task lighting ringed around the Dejarik table, set into the padded banquette. The corridor beyond the lounge glows faint red from bulkhead emergency strips nobody bothered to turn off after the last alert. The holochess board throws a soft blue-green across nearby faces when it idles.
atmosphere.sound: The steady rumble of the hyperdrive in the deck plates, felt as much as heard. The occasional pop and settle of the ship's hull. A faint whine from a coolant line behind the wall that someone has been meaning to fix since before the last job. A low muttering from the cockpit corridor that sounds like the co-pilot losing an argument with himself. The faint electric hum of the Dejarik table idling.
atmosphere.crowd: The lounge was designed to seat a smuggler and whoever he was running for that week. The banquette around the table holds five tightly, six if one of them is short. The working group has the run of it. Nobody else is aboard except the pilots and the droid.
atmosphere.smell: Recycled ship air, a base note of ozone from the reactor vents. The faint oil-and-fur scent from the cockpit corridor that the Wookiee leaves wherever he has been. Warm solder from a panel somebody has been servicing. Tarine tea brewing in the galley alcove. The specific dry-plastic smell of old deck plating that has heated and cooled a hundred thousand times.

layout.holochess_table: A low circular black metal table in the center of the lounge, its polished top scorched at the edges from long play. The holoprojector in its center is currently idling, throwing a soft grid of blue-green light. Two grip-handled seats flank the table. The last match was not completed.
layout.main_banquette: A wraparound bench of dark brown leather, cracked at the seams and repaired in three places with ship tape. Padded gray wall panels above it, dented in one spot where something once hit them hard. Ship blueprints have been taped to the wall above the bench and never taken down.
layout.corridor_to_cockpit: A rounded rectangular doorway leading forward to the cockpit, the corridor lit faint red, pipe-lined, and narrow enough that only one person can pass at a time. The cockpit at the far end is a distant glow of instrument lights and the silhouettes of two pilots. Somebody is arguing.
layout.galley_alcove: A small recessed alcove off the lounge with a rehydrator, a tea dispenser, a couple of packaged meal trays, and a magnetic rack of mismatched utensils. The rehydrator makes a single ding when it is done and is currently silent. A half-empty mug of Tarine tea sits on the counter.
layout.engine_corridor: A grated-floor passage heading aft toward the engine room and the smuggling compartments, warmer than the rest of the ship, smelling of ozone. Toolboxes have been left out along one wall. A protocol droid's disassembled arm is clamped in a vise on a workbench halfway down.

objects_of_note: The Dejarik board, scorched at the edges from years of play, currently idling with a holographic Monnok frozen mid-motion on a green square.
A tool kit left open on the deck beside the banquette, hydrospanner missing from the set.
A pair of mismatched dice hanging from a hook in the corridor to the cockpit, gold-painted metal, one chip scratched off the corner of one die.
A half-empty mug of Tarine tea on the galley alcove counter, still warm. Nobody currently in the lounge is drinking it.
A weapons rack against the aft bulkhead with three empty slots and one filled by a stock-less blaster carbine. The slots do not look recently emptied.
mood: Familiar and compromised. The Falcon is held together by salvage and stubbornness, and its main lounge is a room that knows it has carried people in much worse trouble than this. The hyperdrive rumble reminds everyone that the ship is committed to a course that cannot be reversed until the next drop. Frameworks brought in here have to account for the rattle, the heat off the deck plates, and the distant sound of the pilots arguing about sensor readings. Decisions made in this lounge tend to get made fast. The hull is between the working group and vacuum. The smuggler would prefer that nobody touch anything.
